emergency_definition: Calls from existing builders or G&M Pressure Washing regarding gas station properties (Chevron and Esso).
emergency_routing_rules: Emergency calls from G&M Pressure Washing (specifically Shelley) regarding gas station properties should be patched through directly to Ben.
non_emergency_routing_rules: For general callers after hours, the agent informs them they will be contacted the next business day. During office hours, calls currently forward to Clara if Ben ignores/declines. Once Ben's second number is active, Clara will answer first and transfer to Ben's second line if required.
call_transfer_rules: Clara can transfer calls to Ben's second line (to be activated) if a customer insists or if a specific emergency criteria is met. Forwarding is currently configured for when Ben declines or fails to answer his Android device.
integration_constraints: System must send post-call notifications via email (info@benselectricsolutionsteam.com) and SMS to the business mainline. Call data must be accessible via a web dashboard.
after_hours_flow_summary: Clara answers all incoming calls. Recognized emergencies from G&M Pressure Washing (Shelley) are patched to Ben. Others are informed of a next-business-day callback.
office_hours_flow_summary: Calls currently ring Ben first; if unanswered, Clara handles service fee info ($115 call-out, $98/hr residential), gathers lead info, or transfers back to Ben. Transition plan is to have Clara answer first once Ben has his second personal number.
questions_or_unknowns: Confirmation of second personal phone number activation (expected Friday)
Specific addresses for gas station properties (Shelley usually provides this info on the call)
notes: Service fee: $115 call-out fee (covers getting to site), then $98/hour ($49 per half-hour increment). Clara should only mention fees if specifically asked by the caller. Client is using an Android device and plan to separate personal calls to a second line.
